_source: Captured from the real public Polymarket APIs (gamma-api.polymarket.com, clob.polymarket.com).
_why: Recorded real responses replayed keyless through handlePolymarketRoute so the parser is validated against the real API shape (JSON-string outcomes/clobTokenIds, float volume24hr/bestBid). routes.real.test.ts re-fetches live to detect drift.

gammaMarkets.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 15, 2026?
gammaMarkets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent developments center on the June 14–15, 2026, preliminary memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran, virtually signed by President Trump, Vice President JD Vance, and Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, with formal signing planned for June 19 in Switzerland. Mediated primarily by Pakistan, the framework extends the existing ceasefire, reopens the Strait of Hormuz to commercial traffic, halts hostilities including in Lebanon, and initiates a 60-day negotiation window focused on Iran’s nuclear program, uranium enrichment, sanctions relief, and frozen assets. This follows months of direct conflict and intermittent talks since early 2025, including prior failed rounds and a temporary April ceasefire. Diverging interpretations of terms, Israel’s potential role, and unresolved nuclear issues create uncertainty over whether a comprehensive permanent agreement can be finalized within the defined period.

gammaMarkets.question: Will Argentina win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
gammaMarkets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: World Cup Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France leads the 2026 World Cup winner market at 19.6% implied probability, followed closely by Spain, England, Portugal, and Argentina, because of deep squads featuring elite attackers like Kylian Mbappé and a favorable early group-stage result against Senegal. Spain slipped after a surprising 0-0 draw with Cape Verde that prompted immediate odds movement, while England and Portugal benefit from consistent recent Nations League and qualifying form plus midfield depth. The tight clustering among the top five reflects the expanded 48-team field, balanced European and South American contenders, and the absence of a dominant pre-tournament favorite after warm-up results and opening matches. Multiple teams retain realistic paths through strong group positioning and knockout experience, keeping probabilities compressed as the tournament advances.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent US-Iran talks produced a June 14-15 memorandum of understanding that ends active hostilities, lifts the US naval blockade, and reopens the Strait of Hormuz while scheduling 60 days of further negotiations on Iran’s nuclear program, uranium stockpiles, and sanctions relief. The framework, mediated by Pakistan and others and set for formal signing around June 19 in Geneva, builds on the April ceasefire but stops short of a comprehensive permanent agreement. Divergent interpretations between the parties on implementation details, combined with the pending nuclear track and potential for renewed escalation if talks stall, shape trader assessments of whether a binding long-term deal can be finalized within any near-term resolution window.
